## Description

Creating algorithmic art using p5.js with seeded randomness and interactive parameter exploration. Generate original algorithmic art, flow fields, and particle systems.

## When to Use

Use this skill when:

- Creating art using code
- Generating algorithmic art
- Building flow fields
- Creating particle systems
- Exploring generative design
- Building interactive visualizations

**Triggers**: "Create algorithmic art", "Build a generative art piece", "Make a flow field", "Create a particle system", "Generate art with code"

## When NOT to Use

- For copying existing artists' work (avoid copyright violations)
- For static design (use canvas-design instead)
- For web UI (use frontend-design instead)
- For data visualization (use mermaid-diagrams instead)

## Workflow

### Phase 1: Concept

- Define artistic intent
- Choose algorithm type
- Plan parameter space
- Sketch initial design

### Phase 2: Implementation

- Set up p5.js canvas
- Implement core algorithm
- Add randomness with seeding
- Create parameter controls

### Phase 3: Refinement

- Adjust colors and shapes
- Optimize performance
- Add interactivity
- Test parameter ranges

### Phase 4: Export

- Generate final artwork
- Export as PNG or SVG
- Document parameters
- Share results

## Tools & References

**Related Skills**:

- canvas-design - Create visual art
- frontend-design - Build interactive UI
- theme-factory - Apply themes

**Libraries**:

- p5.js - Creative coding library
- Perlin noise - Natural randomness
- Color theory - Color selection

## Skills Required

| Skill | Purpose |
|-------|---------|
| `canvas-design` | Visual art and design principles |
| `frontend-design` | Interactive UI implementation |

## Best Practices

- **Seeded Randomness**: Use seeds for reproducibility
- **Parameter Exploration**: Make art interactive
- **Performance**: Optimize for smooth animation
- **Originality**: Create original work, don't copy
- **Documentation**: Document algorithm and parameters

## Verification Checklist

- [ ] Artistic intent and algorithm type defined
- [ ] p5.js canvas set up and rendering
- [ ] Core algorithm implemented with seeded randomness
- [ ] Parameter controls working
- [ ] Final artwork exported (PNG/SVG)
- [ ] Parameters documented for reproducibility

## Pitfalls

- Using copyrighted artists' work without transformation violates copyright
- Unseeded randomness makes results irreproducible
- Complex particle systems without performance optimization cause frame drops
- Over-exporting at wrong resolution reduces quality
